title: Find All Anagrams
description: Write a function that finds all starting indices of anagram substrings in a given string. An anagram is a word formed by rearranging the letters of another word. This exercise combines string manipulation with algorithmic thinking.
instructions: Create a function named `findAnagrams` that accepts two strings: s (text) and p (pattern)
Find all starting indices where an anagram of p appears as a substring in s
An anagram means the substring contains the same characters with the same frequency
Return an array of starting indices (0-indexed)
Both strings will only contain lowercase letters

hints.content: Use a sliding window approach with a frequency map. Create a map of character counts for the pattern, then slide a window of the same size across the text.
For each window position, maintain a frequency map of characters in the current window. Compare this map with the pattern's frequency map to check if they match.
Create helper function to compare two maps. As you slide the window, add the new character and remove the leftmost character efficiently. Store the starting index when maps match.
